api_key.description: Calendly personal access token or OAuth token. Used only for Bearer auth (Authorization: Bearer <api_key>); never logged.

organization_uri.description: The authenticated user's Calendly organization URI (e.g. https://api.calendly.com/organizations/AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA), used to scope every organization-level list request (organization=<organization_uri>). Legacy discovers this dynamically on every read via GET /users/me (current_organization); the engine dialect has no mechanism to chain a prior request's response into a later request's query params, so this bundle asks the operator to configure the (per-account invariant) URI once instead — resolvable by calling GET https://api.calendly.com/users/me and reading resource.current_organization. See docs.md's Known limits.

base_url.description: Calendly API base URL override for tests or proxies.

start_date.description: RFC3339 lower bound; only scheduled_events starting at or after this time are read (Calendly's min_start_time filter). Other streams have no server-side lower-bound filter, matching legacy.

page_size.description: Records per page (1-100), sent as the count query param.

user_uri.description: The Calendly user URI whose availability schedules the user_availability_schedules stream reads (Calendly's `user` query filter is a required parameter naming one specific user, not the whole organization). Same per-account-invariant-value pattern as organization_uri: Calendly's API gives no 'list every user' endpoint and the engine dialect cannot chain a prior request's response into this one, so the operator configures it once — resolvable by calling GET https://api.calendly.com/users/me and reading resource.uri (the authenticated user's own URI is the common case). Required only for the user_availability_schedules stream; every other stream in this bundle is unaffected if it is left unset. See docs.md's Known limits.

routing_form_uri.description: The Calendly routing form URI whose submissions the routing_form_submissions stream reads (Calendly's `routing_form` query filter is a required parameter naming one specific form, not the whole organization). Resolvable from the routing_forms stream's own uri field. Required only for the routing_form_submissions stream; every other stream in this bundle is unaffected if it is left unset. See docs.md's Known limits.
